summary: Provision Cognito for a new sign-up/sign-in surface: the user pool that stores identities, an app client that talks to it, and an identity pool that vends temporary AWS credentials to signed-in users. All three are engineer-gated creates — a pool anchors an entire application's authentication and is expensive to redo, so nothing here is blind self-service.

operations.title: Create a new user pool
operations.summary: Request a new Cognito user pool for sign-up and sign-in. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Specify a small user directory: its name, MFA posture, which contact channels auto-verify at sign-up, and one account-recovery mechanism. Lambda triggers, custom schema attributes, and the email/SMS sending configuration are always finished by the engineer, never captured here. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: pool_name
params.label: Pool name
params.help: The pool's name in the Cognito console.

params.name: mfa_configuration
params.label: MFA
params.help: ON requires every user to enroll a second factor; OPTIONAL lets each user choose; OFF disables it entirely.

params.name: deletion_protection
params.label: Deletion protection
params.help: ACTIVE blocks an accidental terraform destroy of this pool; INACTIVE allows it.

params.name: auto_verified_attributes
params.label: Auto-verified contact channels
params.help: Cognito sends a verification code to these channels at sign-up. Leave blank to verify nothing automatically.

params.name: recovery_mechanism_name
params.label: Account recovery method
params.help: How a user proves ownership of the account to reset a forgotten password.

params.name: recovery_mechanism_priority
params.label: Recovery method priority
params.help: Lower number is tried first. Cognito allows up to two recovery mechanisms; this form seeds one.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Pool name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this pool is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this pool.

decisions: Lambda triggers (custom auth challenge, pre-token generation, and the rest of lambda_config) are engineer decisions — none are wired here
Custom schema attributes and the email/SMS sending configuration (a verified SES identity or SNS role) are engineer follow-ups
This form seeds one recovery mechanism at priority 1; add a second (e.g. verified_phone_number as a priority-2 fallback) as an engineer follow-up — Cognito allows up to two
Password policy keeps Cognito's built-in defaults (8-character minimum, upper/lower/number/symbol required); tighten or relax it as an engineer follow-up

operations.title: Create a new app client
operations.summary: Request a new app client for an existing user pool. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Specify a new aws_cognito_user_pool_client bound to an existing pool: whether it holds a confidential client secret, which direct auth flows it's allowed to use, and its user-enumeration protection. Hosted UI / OAuth wiring, token validity overrides, and attribute read/write allowlists are always finished by the engineer. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: client_name
params.label: App client name
params.help: The app client's name in the Cognito console.

params.name: user_pool
params.label: User pool
params.help: The user pool this app client belongs to. Create the pool first (cognito-pool-create) if the list is empty.

params.name: generate_secret
params.label: Generate a client secret
params.help: On for a confidential client (a server-side app that can protect a secret); off for a public client (SPA or mobile app) — a client secret must never ship in browser or mobile code.

params.name: explicit_auth_flows
params.label: Allowed auth flows
params.help: Which direct (non-Hosted-UI) sign-in flows this client may use. SRP is the standard secure default; USER_PASSWORD_AUTH sends the password directly and should stay off unless a legacy client needs it.

params.name: prevent_user_existence_errors
params.label: Hide account-existence on failed sign-in
params.help: ENABLED (recommended) gives the same error whether a username exists or not; LEGACY reveals which usernames are registered.

decisions: Hosted UI / OAuth configuration (callback_urls, logout_urls, allowed_oauth_flows/scopes, supported_identity_providers) is an engineer follow-up if this client uses the Hosted UI rather than direct SRP/password auth
Token validity periods keep Cognito's defaults (60 minutes access/ID, 30 days refresh); tune them afterward if needed
Read/write attribute allowlists (read_attributes/write_attributes) default to every standard attribute; narrow them as an engineer follow-up if this client should see less of the user profile

operations.title: Create a new identity pool
operations.summary: Request a new Cognito identity pool for temporary AWS credentials. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Specify a new aws_cognito_identity_pool shell: its name and whether unauthenticated (guest) access is allowed. Wiring it to a login provider and attaching IAM roles are always finished by the engineer — an identity pool with no provider and no role attachment cannot vend credentials yet. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: identity_pool_name
params.label: Identity pool name
params.help: The identity pool's name in the Cognito console.

params.name: allow_unauthenticated_identities
params.label: Allow guest (unauthenticated) access
params.help: On lets callers with no sign-in obtain temporary AWS credentials under the unauthenticated role — keep off unless the app genuinely needs guest access.

params.name: allow_classic_flow
params.label: Allow classic (basic) auth flow
params.help: Legacy credential-vending flow. Leave off unless an old SDK integration specifically needs it.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Identity pool name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this identity pool is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this identity pool.

decisions: Wiring this identity pool to a login provider (a Cognito user pool client, SAML, OIDC, or social login) is an engineer follow-up — this form provisions the identity pool shell with no providers attached yet
IAM roles for authenticated/unauthenticated identities (the companion aws_cognito_identity_pool_roles_attachment) are an engineer decision — without it this pool cannot vend credentials
